compiled_state.author: Claude

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (round 4). Advisory triage: E1 (AC5/AC6 share a -k proof) is cosmetic — both ACs are satisfied by DISTINCT test cases in test_completion_gate.py (test_resolve_session_captures_claude_code_session_id proves the AC5 bug-fix; the four updated existing cases prove AC6); splitting the filter is wording-only and re-editing would invalidate the fresh attestation. G1G2 (stale CLAUDE.md fingerprint order) fixed in the implementation commit (code change, not a ticket edit).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Today rebar has TWO session resolvers with DIFFERENT precedence chains:
`session_log._resolve_session_fp` (`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> REBAR_SESSION_ID ->
SESSION_ID -> None`) and `transition_close._resolve_session` (`REBAR_SESSION_ID ->
SESSION_ID -> git HEAD -> "unknown"`). The latter OMITS `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, so in a
real Claude Code session the FORCE_CLOSE audit comment falls through to git HEAD — a value
that changes every commit and is not a session id. This is a latent bug, and the two must
converge onto ONE shared resolver before anything else in the epic builds on session
resolution.

## What
Introduce one shared `resolve_session_id()` helper in a NEW module
`src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py`, implementing a single **data-driven, ordered** var
list so new harness vars are a one-line add:

    _SESSION_ID_VARS = ["REBAR_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID"]

Precedence rationale: the explicit, rebar-owned `REBAR_SESSION_ID` wins first (an
operator/hook override is authoritative), then the native harness var
`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, then the ambient `SESSION_ID`, else `None`.

- **Empty-string handling (DECISION):** a var whose value is empty / whitespace-only is
  treated as ABSENT (skipped), matching the existing falsy-`or`-chain semantics. Only the
  first *non-empty* var wins; all-empty / all-absent -> `None`.
- **Refactor both call sites onto it:**
  - `session_log._resolve_session_fp()` -> `return resolve_session_id()`. This
    **intentionally inverts** session_log's current precedence (today `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`
    before `REBAR_SESSION_ID`; now `REBAR_SESSION_ID` first). Impact is limited to sessions
    that set BOTH vars (rare); the unified explicit-rebar-first contract is deliberate and is
    documented in `docs/config.md`.
  - `transition_close._resolve_session(tracker)` -> `resolve_session_id() or
    _short_head(tracker) or "unknown"`. The shared resolver returns `None` on absence; the
    git-HEAD / `"unknown"` fallback stays LOCAL to this call site (a cosmetic non-empty
    audit string). This FIXES THE BUG: `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is now captured at the
    FORCE_CLOSE audit site.
- **AC4 scope (DECISION):** "never returns git HEAD" constrains the **return value of
  `resolve_session_id()` ONLY**. The FORCE_CLOSE audit comment MAY still render short HEAD
  via the documented local cosmetic fallback — that is out of scope for the shared resolver.
- **Deferred to S5 (c557):** the OSS harness vars `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` / `CODEX_THREAD_ID`
  are added to the list by story `privy-eloquent-nauplius`, which owns verifying their
  canonical names + harness provenance. S1 ships the extensible list with the three vars
  above only.
- Update the existing `_resolve_session` unit tests in
  `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py` (~lines 477-504) that call the
  private helper, so they reflect the delegation (and guard against an ambient
  `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` by `delenv`-ing it in the fallback cases).
- The enumerated consumer `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py` is
  UNAFFECTED by the precedence inversion and needs NO code change: every fingerprinting case
  sets `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` only after `delenv`-ing `REBAR_SESSION_ID`/`SESSION_ID`, so
  no assertion depends on which of REBAR vs CLAUDE_CODE wins (they are never both set). It is
  simply re-run to prove it stays green after the refactor.

## Scope
- IN: new `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` shared helper + ordered list; refactor of the
  two existing resolvers; unit tests for precedence / empty-string / all-absent + a
  call-site wiring test proving `transition_close` captures `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` + a
  no-duplicate-chains source guard test; update of the `test_completion_gate.py` resolver
  tests and a green re-run of the (unmodified) `test_session_log_capture.py` consumer;
  rewrite of the existing `docs/config.md` "Session provenance" section.
- OUT: recording the id on claims (S2 / 68ef); `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`/`CODEX_THREAD_ID` +
  harness provenance (S5 / c557); reducer / read / hook work (later stories).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resolve_session_id()` returns the first NON-EMPTY var in order
      `REBAR_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID`, else `None`.
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_id.py -q` (cases assert each var's precedence).
- [ ] An empty-string / whitespace-only value is skipped (treated as absent).
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_id.py -k empty -q`.
- [ ] `resolve_session_id()` NEVER returns git HEAD (its var list contains no git call).
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_id.py -k "all_absent or none" -q` asserts
      `None` when all vars are unset (no HEAD value can appear).
- [ ] `session_log._resolve_session_fp` and `transition_close._resolve_session` BOTH
      delegate to the shared helper AND the OLD duplicate env-lookup chains are REMOVED.
      Proof: a source-guard test in `test_session_id.py` reads both source files and asserts
      each contains `resolve_session_id` AND contains NO `os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID"`,
      `os.environ.get("REBAR_SESSION_ID"`, or `os.environ.get("SESSION_ID"` literal (the only
      session-var env reads live in `session_id.py`).
- [ ] FORCE_CLOSE audit captures `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` when set (the bug fix).
      Proof: `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py -k resolve_session -q`
      includes a case that sets ONLY `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` and asserts
      `transition_close._resolve_session(tracker)` returns that value (not HEAD).
- [ ] Existing `_resolve_session` tests updated and green.
      Proof: `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py -k resolve_session -q`.
- [ ] The unmodified `test_session_log_capture.py` consumer stays green after the refactor.
      Proof: `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py -q`.
- [ ] The existing `docs/config.md` "Session provenance" section (currently documenting
      `REBAR_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID -> short git HEAD -> "unknown"`) is rewritten to the
      unified ordered contract, ADDING `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` in its correct position and a
      one-line precedence-inversion note; the stale `SESSION_ID -> short git HEAD` chain (as
      the resolver contract) no longer appears. Proof: a doc-content assertion in
      `test_session_id.py` that reads `docs/config.md` and asserts the new ordered list string
      is present AND the exact stale contract string is absent.

## Depends on
- (none — foundation story)
compiled_state.title: Unify session-id resolution into one shared, ordered resolver (+ fix FORCE_CLOSE Claude Code session bug)
